message:
fix: bd list --status all showing 0 issues (bd-148)

The status filter was treating 'all' as a literal status value instead of
a special case meaning 'show all statuses'. This caused the SQL query to
filter for 'WHERE status = all' which matched no issues.

Fixed by checking if status is 'all' and skipping the filter in that case.

- Fix list.go to skip status filter when status == 'all'
- Update CHANGELOG with fix details
- All tests pass

Default behavior: fail on collision (safe).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-16T20:46:08.971822-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T01:32:00.645323-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-16T10:07:34.003238-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-14","depends_on_id":"bd-48","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-16T21:51:08.923374-07:00","created_by":"renumber"}]}
 {"id":"bd-144","title":"Parent with desc","description":"See [deleted:bd-143] for details","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-17T21:11:37.608527-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T21:11:42.32958-07:00"}
+{"id":"bd-148","title":"bd list shows 0 issues despite database containing 115 issues","description":"When running 'bd list --status all' it shows 'Found 0 issues' even though 'bd stats' shows 115 total issues and 'sqlite3 .beads/vc.db "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues"' returns 115.

Reproduction:
1. cd ~/src/vc/vc
2. bd stats  # Shows 115 issues
3. bd list --status all  # Shows 0 issues
4. sqlite3 .beads/vc.db 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues;'  # Shows 115

Expected: bd list should show all 115 issues
Actual: Shows 'Found 0 issues:'

This occurs with both /opt/homebrew/bin/bd (v0.9.9) and ~/src/vc/adar/beads/bd (v0.9.10)","design":"Possible causes:
- Default filter excluding all issues
- Database query issue in list command
- Auto-discovery finding wrong database (but stats works?)
- Recent deletion operation corrupted some index","acceptance_criteria":"bd list --status all shows all issues that bd stats counts","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-17T21:19:08.225181-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T21:55:40.788625-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-17T21:55:40.788625-07:00"}
+{"id":"bd-149","title":"Confusing version mismatch warnings with contradictory messages","description":"The version mismatch warning shows contradictory messages depending on which binary version is used:

When using v0.9.10 binary with v0.9.9 database:
'Your bd binary (v0.9.10) differs from the database version (v0.9.9)'
'Your binary appears to be OUTDATED.'

When using v0.9.9 binary with v0.9.10 database:
'Your bd binary (v0.9.9) differs from the database version (v0.9.10)'
'Your binary appears NEWER than the database.'

The first message is incorrect - v0.9.10 > v0.9.9, so the binary is NEWER, not outdated.

Reproduction:
1. Use ~/src/vc/adar/beads/bd (v0.9.10) with a v0.9.9 database
2. Observe warning says binary is OUTDATED when it's actually newer

Expected: Correct version comparison
Actual: Inverted comparison logic","design":"Fix version comparison in warning message generation. Should compare semantic versions correctly.","acceptance_criteria":"Warning correctly identifies which component (binary vs database) is newer/older","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-17T21:19:19.540274-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T21:19:19.540274-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-15","title":"Write comprehensive collision resolution tests","description":"Test cases: simple collision, multiple collisions, dependency updates, text reference updates, chain dependencies, edge cases (partial ID matches, case sensitivity, triple merges). Add to import_test.go and collision_test.go.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-16T20:46:08.971822-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T01:32:00.647268-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-16T10:07:34.007864-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15","depends_on_id":"bd-48","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-16T21:51:08.917092-07:00","created_by":"renumber"}]}
+{"id":"bd-150","title":"bd renumber removes issues with different prefix than majority","description":"The 'bd renumber' command appears to remove issues that have a different prefix than the majority of issues.

Reproduction:
1. Database has 115 vc- issues and 2 bd- issues (bd-118, bd-119)
2. Run: bd renumber --force
3. Result: Only vc- issues remain, bd-118 and bd-119 are deleted

Expected: All issues should be renumbered, regardless of prefix
Actual: Issues with minority prefix are removed

Evidence:
- Before renumber: 117 issues total (115 vc-, 2 bd-)
- After renumber: 115 issues total (115 vc-, 0 bd-)
- bd-118 was 'Fix compilation errors in mission orchestrator test' (closed)
- bd-119 was 'Fix bool pointer literal errors' (in_progress)

This is data loss - active work (bd-119 was in_progress) was silently deleted.","design":"Renumber should handle mixed prefixes:
1. Group issues by prefix
2. Renumber each group independently
3. Or: fail if multiple prefixes detected with helpful error
4. Or: add --prefix flag to specify which prefix to renumber

Never silently delete issues.","acceptance_criteria":"Renumber preserves all issues regardless of prefix, or fails with clear error message","notes":"Not a bug - mixed prefixes in one database is user error. Filed bd-151 for proper validation instead.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-17T21:43:07.800716-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T21:46:26.813508-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-17T21:46:26.813512-07:00"}
+{"id":"bd-151","title":"Validate issue prefix matches database prefix on create","description":"Users can accidentally create issues with the wrong prefix (e.g., creating 'bd-118' in the vc tracker instead of the beads tracker).

This causes problems:
- Issues appear in wrong project
- Renumbering silently removes them (correct behavior, but surprising)
- Confusion about which tracker owns the issue

Evidence from vc project:
- bd-118 and bd-119 were created in ~/src/vc/vc/.beads/vc.db (should use vc- prefix)
- These were silently removed during renumbering (working as intended)
- But user didn't realize they were in wrong database until too late

Root cause: User was in vc directory but created issues with bd- prefix, probably because they were beads-related fixes.","design":"Options:

1. **Strict validation (recommended)**:
   - Detect database prefix from existing issues or .beads/*.db filename
   - Reject 'bd create' if prefix doesn't match
   - Error: 'This database uses prefix vc-, but you tried to create bd-X. Use --force to override.'

2. **Auto-correct prefix**:
   - 'bd create' in vc database always uses vc- prefix regardless of what user intended
   - Warning: 'Auto-corrected prefix from bd- to vc-'

3. **Multiple prefix support**:
   - Allow multiple prefixes per database
   - Group by prefix during renumbering
   - Complexity not worth it

Prefer option 1 - fail fast with clear error.","acceptance_criteria":"Creating an issue with wrong prefix fails with helpful error message explaining the mismatch","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-10-17T21:46:27.204648-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T21:46:27.204648-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-16","title":"Update documentation for collision resolution","description":"Update README.md with collision resolution section. Update CLAUDE.md with new workflow. Document --resolve-collisions and --dry-run flags. Add example scenarios showing branch merge workflows.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-16T20:46:08.971822-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T01:32:00.648113-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-16T10:07:34.028648-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-16","depends_on_id":"bd-48","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-16T21:51:08.924312-07:00","created_by":"renumber"}]}
 {"id":"bd-17","title":"bd should auto-detect .beads/*.db in current directory","description":"When bd is run without --db flag, it defaults to beads' own database instead of looking for a .beads/*.db file in the current working directory. This causes confusion when working on other projects that use beads for issue tracking (like vc).

Expected behavior: bd should search for .beads/*.db in cwd and use that if found, before falling back to default beads database.

Example: Running 'bd ready' in /Users/stevey/src/vc/vc/ should automatically find and use .beads/vc.db without requiring --db flag every time.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-16T20:46:08.971822-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T01:32:00.650584-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-16T10:07:34.046944-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-18","title":"Document or automate JSONL sync workflow for git collaboration","description":"When using beads across multiple machines/environments via git, there's a workflow gap:

1. Machine A: Create issues → stored in .beads/project.db
2. Machine A: bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl
3. Machine A: git add .beads/issues.jsonl && git commit && git push
4. Machine B: git pull
5. Machine B: ??? issues.jsonl exists but project.db is empty/stale

The missing step is: bd import --db .beads/project.db -i .beads/issues.jsonl

This needs to be either:
a) Documented clearly in workflow docs
b) Automated (e.g., git hook, or bd auto-imports if jsonl is newer than db)
c) Both

Real-world impact: User had Claude Code on GCP VM create vc issues from BOOTSTRAP.md. They were exported to issues.jsonl and committed. But on local machine, vc.db was empty until manual import was run.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-16T20:46:08.971822-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-17T01:32:00.651438-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-14T02:51:52.199766-07:00"}

@@ -17,6 +17,11 @@ and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0
   - Atomic transactions: all deletions succeed or none do
   - Comprehensive statistics: tracks deleted issues, dependencies, labels, and events
 
+### Fixed
+- **Critical**: `bd list --status all` showing 0 issues (bd-148)
+  - Status filter now treats "all" as special value meaning "show all statuses"
+  - Previously treated "all" as literal status value, matching no issues
+
